meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master SaaS Development as a Freelancer
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with SaaS architecture. Use this expert checklist to ship production-ready apps using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for freelancers.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the lack of SaaS knowledge requires moving away from manual coding toward high-leverage starter kits. By combining Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can bypass months of learning, automate your backend infrastructure, and ship professional-grade SaaS products for clients in record time.
content.intro:
The client is waiting, the deadline is aggressive, and the empty IDE staring back at you is a reminder of the chasm between 'knowing how to code' and 'shipping a SaaS.' Most freelancers stall because they try to reinvent the authentication, database schema, and payment integration wheel for every single project. It’s a recipe for burnout and scope creep.

But the game has changed. You no longer need to be a full-stack architect to build scalable software. By leveraging modern AI agents and pre-configured starter kits, you can collapse months of development into days. This guide provides the blueprint to transform your technical limitations into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Core Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, establish your database relationships. Use Antigravity to generate a robust schema that handles multi-tenancy from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a multi-tenant SaaS database schema using Prisma. Include tables for Users, Organizations, Subscriptions, and Features. Ensure Row Level Security is configured for PostgreSQL.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize Prisma schema with multi-tenancy support'

steps.title: Bootstrap Authentication Flow
steps.description: Auth is the highest-risk area of any SaaS. Use a proven library to handle sessions, magic links, and role-based access control.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Clerk authentication integration with a Next.js 14 app. Create middleware to protect dashboard routes and sync user data to the database on signup.

steps.title: Configure Stripe Subscription Tiers
steps.description: Don't build billing from scratch. Hook your starter kit into Stripe Webhooks to manage subscription lifecycle states automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler for subscription creation, upgrades, and cancellations. Update the 'subscription_status' field in the database based on webhook events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup Stripe webhook endpoint and test with CLI'

steps.title: Automate API Route Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to standardize your API responses, ensuring all endpoints follow a consistent error-handling and data-validation pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a base API utility for Next.js that enforces Zod validation and consistent JSON response structures for all CRUD operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate boilerplate API routes with Zod validation'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Infrastructure
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD pipeline to ensure every commit is tested and deployed without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file with environment variable management for production and staging environments.

pro_tips: Always define your Zod schemas first; let your database and API types derive from them to eliminate sync errors.
Use 'feature flags' in your starter kit to toggle beta features for specific client organizations without redeploying code.
When using Claude Code, always provide a 'context file' listing your project's directory structure so the agent understands your file naming conventions.
Keep your database migrations small and frequent to prevent 'migration hell' during client handovers.
Standardize your logging early; use a service like Axiom or Logtail to debug production issues before the client reports them.
content.next_steps: After deployment, set up automated monitoring and create a 'Developer README' file to ensure your client can manage the app after you hand off the project.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to dominate the freelance market. Stop guessing and start shipping with a system designed for scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
